title: digitalis purpurea f. humilis
intro: Digitalis purpurea f. humilis, also known as the foxglove or dwarf foxglove, is a variety of the digitalis purpurea plant that is characterized by its shorter height and smaller flowers. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea f. humilis are derived from its ability to increase the force of the heart's contractions and slow its rate, making it a valuable treatment for heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiac function, and reduce the risk of heart failure, among others. In herbal preparations, Digitalis purpurea f. humilis is often used in tinctures, teas, and extracts, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential for toxicity and side effects.
